intro: Chilita boedekeriana, also known as Boedeker's Chile, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating infections. The bioactive constituents of Chilita boedekeriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
